traces.section: Card Age And Base Card Features
traces.mapping_rationale: The notebook treats year and month from `first_active_month` as the cleanest first card-age block.

traces.section: Card Age And Base Card Features
traces.mapping_rationale: Elapsed card age stays in the final selection because it is one of the most repeated and interpretable Elo base-table features.
The shortlist kept simple dummy expansion for the three low-cardinality base-card features, and this row is the bank-aligned representative for `feature_1`.
The same frozen base-card encoding shortlist maps `feature_2` to its current benchmark id here.
The final selection keeps the full low-cardinality base-card encoding block, including `feature_3`.

traces.section: Transaction Tables, Recency, And Aggregation Direction
traces.mapping_rationale: I treated `month_diff` as the clearest recurring recency feature before any card-level aggregation.

traces.section: Transaction Tables, Recency, And Aggregation Direction
traces.mapping_rationale: Historical numeric transaction aggregation is one of the two card-level summary blocks I kept in the final selection.

traces.section: Transaction Tables, Recency, And Aggregation Direction
traces.mapping_rationale: The new-merchant numeric aggregation block is kept as the matching companion to the historical numeric summary path.

omitted_steps.note: The manual pass stops before the transaction-flag cleanup branch, category_2/category_3 dummy expansion, categorical aggregate blocks, and diversity-count aggregates.
